summary: Possible typosquat of postcss-minify-selectors with lookalike dependency
details: The package name 'postcss-minify-selector' (singular) closely resembles the widely-used cssnano plugin 'postcss-minify-selectors' (plural). It also declares a dependency on 'postcss-minify-selector-parser', which resembles the legitimate 'postcss-selector-parser'. The local source is a benign PostCSS plugin: src/index.js performs selector-string transformations and requires 'postcss-minify-selector-parser/cjs-runner'; there are no install/lifecycle hooks, no network I/O, no obfuscation, and no credential reads in this package itself. Any installer risk would come from the lookalike transitive dependency, which is resolved separately. Name-similarity alone, without confirmed harm payload in the resolved dep, is not sufficient to block — routing to human review for name-confusion assessment and evaluation of the suspicious dependency.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
